budgetPolicy.priceBasis: Public list prices accessed on 2026-07-11; taxes, negotiated discounts, data-residency uplifts, storage, network egress, moderation, and support are excluded unless stated.

modalities.budgetExample: 100 medium 1024x1024 GPT Image 2 outputs are about USD 5.30 before input-token and tax costs.

failover.activation: Backup may be enabled only after its own legal, credential, budget, and staging approvals are complete.
failover.trigger: Primary circuit open for 5 minutes after at least 3 retryable failures or a confirmed provider incident.

replacementTriggers: model retirement or incompatible API change
30-day p95 latency above 120 seconds
7-day successful-generation rate below 97 percent
effective cost above USD 0.25 per accepted output
loss of commercial-use, retention, or supported-region eligibility

modalities.budgetExample: A 2,000-input-token and 1,000-output-token GPT-5.6 Terra turn is about USD 0.020 at short-context standard rates; 1,000 such turns are about USD 20.

failover.activation: Backup may be enabled only after prompt, tool, safety, and response-schema parity tests pass under a separate approval.
failover.trigger: Primary circuit open for 2 minutes after at least 5 retryable failures, sustained 429s, or a confirmed provider incident.

replacementTriggers: model retirement or material prompt/tool incompatibility
30-day p95 time-to-first-token above 5 seconds
7-day successful-turn rate below 99 percent
evaluated task quality drops below the accepted regression threshold
effective cost above USD 0.10 per accepted turn
loss of commercial-use, retention, or supported-region eligibility

modalities.budgetExample: An 8-second Veo 3.1 Fast output is about USD 0.80; 20 outputs are about USD 16 before storage, egress, tax, and support costs.

failover.activation: Runway remains disabled until a no-training enterprise amendment, retention terms, and independent staging approval are recorded.
failover.trigger: Primary circuit open for 15 minutes after 3 terminal provider failures or a confirmed provider incident.

replacementTriggers: model retirement or preview-only regression
30-day p95 completion time above 10 minutes
7-day successful-generation rate below 95 percent
effective cost above USD 1.20 per accepted 8-second output
loss of C2PA, commercial-use, retention, or supported-region eligibility

modalities.budgetExample: A 3-minute Eleven Music output is about USD 0.45; 20 outputs are about USD 9 before tax, storage, egress, and enterprise-contract adjustments.

failover.activation: Lyria 3 Pro may be enabled only after Preview risk, region, quota, retention, output-rights, and migration behavior are accepted in writing.
failover.trigger: Primary circuit open for 15 minutes after 3 terminal provider failures or a confirmed provider incident.

replacementTriggers: model retirement or license-table change
30-day p95 completion time above 10 minutes
7-day successful-generation rate below 95 percent
effective cost above USD 0.60 per accepted 3-minute output
loss of reseller, streaming, media, commercial-use, retention, or supported-region eligibility

api.cancellation: abort the client request; no durable provider job cancellation is documented
api.artifactHandling: base64 output must be copied immediately into governed application storage

pricing.notes: Text input is USD 5.00 per 1M tokens and image input is USD 8.00 per 1M tokens; higher quality can cost materially more.

rights.outputOwnership: Customer owns Output as between Customer and OpenAI, to the extent permitted by law.
rights.trainingUse: API Customer Content is not used to train or improve models unless the customer explicitly opts in.
restrictions: usage policies and safety filters apply

data.defaultRetention: Up to 30 days in abuse-monitoring logs; no application-state retention for image generation and edits.
data.zeroRetention: Eligible after OpenAI approval for GPT Image models, subject to image safety-review exceptions.
data.residency: Image endpoints support regional storage/processing in listed regions, but gpt-image-2 was not listed in the reviewed model-specific residency row; production residency requires written confirmation.

regions.productionCondition: Run only from an explicitly supported deployment and user-access footprint.

limits.published: Tier 1: 100,000 TPM and 5 images per minute; higher tiers scale to 250 images per minute.

sla.publicCommitment: No pay-as-you-go image-model uptime SLA was established from the reviewed public documentation.
sla.productionCondition: Record contracted support/SLA or formally accept best-effort service with the backup path tested.

appControls: human review for reportable or ambiguous content

goLiveConditions: legal approves Services Agreement, DPA, output rights, and supported-country footprint
gpt-image-2 residency and ZDR eligibility are confirmed for the production project
organization verification, account tier, spend limits, and project limits are recorded
staging quality, safety, latency, budget, and governed-storage evidence passes
the exact real-call approval package is completed

api.cancellation: prediction cancellation/deadline supported after start with usage billed through cancellation
api.artifactHandling: copy output before the one-hour API retention window expires

pricing.notes: Public model list price; failed or partial runs may still incur compute charges depending on failure point.

rights.outputOwnership: Replicate assigns its rights in Output to the customer, subject to all model-specific third-party terms.
rights.trainingUse: No sufficiently explicit no-training commitment was established from the reviewed public pages.
restrictions: FLUX API terms and end-user flow-down apply
human copyright review may be required

data.defaultRetention: API prediction inputs, outputs, files, values, and logs are removed after one hour by default; web predictions are retained indefinitely.
data.zeroRetention: No public zero-retention commitment was established.
data.residency: No customer-selectable data-residency commitment was established from the reviewed public pages.

documented: subject to US export controls and provider account eligibility
regions.productionCondition: Legal must approve the customer and end-user geography before traffic.

limits.published: 600 prediction-creation requests per minute and 3,000 requests per minute for other endpoints; actual model throughput can queue.

sla.publicCommitment: Public terms disclaim uninterrupted service; enterprise pricing advertises optional performance SLAs.
sla.productionCondition: Obtain an enterprise SLA or explicitly accept best-effort backup operation.

goLiveConditions: Black Forest Labs and Replicate terms are approved with required end-user disclosures
DPA, no-training position, regions, and SLA/support are confirmed
the existing fixture-only shell is replaced only in an approved adapter task
webhook signatures, polling leases, deadlines, and one-hour artifact capture pass staging
an independent budget and exact real-call approval are recorded

api.polling: not required when background mode is disabled
api.cancellation: abort stream/request and mark the application turn canceled
api.artifactHandling: store only governed application conversation state; send store=false

pricing.notes: Prompts above 272K input tokens use higher long-context rates.

rights.outputOwnership: Customer owns Output as between Customer and OpenAI, to the extent permitted by law.
rights.trainingUse: API Customer Content is not used to train or improve models unless the customer explicitly opts in.
restrictions: outputs require accuracy review where material

data.defaultRetention: Up to 30 days in abuse-monitoring logs. Responses application state is avoided with store=false; background mode is disabled for V1.
data.zeroRetention: Responses is eligible after approval, with store forced false under ZDR.
data.residency: Regional storage is available in listed regions; regional processing for gpt-5.6-terra is documented for the US and Europe.

regions.productionCondition: Run only from an explicitly supported deployment and user-access footprint.

limits.published: Tier 1: 500 RPM, 500,000 TPM, and a 1,500,000-token batch queue.

sla.publicCommitment: No default pay-as-you-go model SLA was established from the reviewed public documentation.
sla.productionCondition: Record Scale/Priority or enterprise terms if an uptime commitment is required.

goLiveConditions: legal approves Services Agreement, DPA, retention controls, and supported-country footprint
ZDR or Modified Abuse Monitoring and store=false are configured as required
prompt, tool, streaming, safety, and evaluation baselines pass
account tier, spend limits, rate limits, and kill switch are recorded
the exact real-call approval package is completed

api.cancellation: abort stream/request and mark the application turn canceled

pricing.notes: The matrix uses regular rates, not the temporary USD 2/USD 10 introductory rate ending 2026-08-31.

rights.outputOwnership: Customer retains Inputs and owns Outputs, to the extent permitted by law.
rights.trainingUse: Commercial Customer Content is not used for model training by default.

data.defaultRetention: API inputs and outputs are deleted within 30 days, except policy, legal, longer-service, or agreed-retention cases.
data.zeroRetention: Available by agreement for eligible products.
data.residency: Traffic may route through the US, Europe, Asia, or Australia; data is stored in the US by default.
data.exceptions: Flagged content may be retained up to 2 years, safety scores up to 7 years, and feedback up to 5 years.

regions.productionCondition: Legal must approve supported-country access and US storage.

limits.published: Start tier Sonnet 5: 1,000 RPM, 2,000,000 input TPM, and 400,000 output TPM.

sla.publicCommitment: Commercial terms provide the service as-is and do not warrant uninterrupted operation.
sla.productionCondition: Record Priority/custom terms or formally accept best-effort backup service.

goLiveConditions: commercial terms, DPA, retention exceptions, US storage, and supported countries are approved
zero-retention eligibility is decided before sensitive content is allowed
prompt, tool, streaming, safety, and response-schema parity tests pass
account limits, spend cap, and kill switch are recorded
an independent staging and real-call approval is completed

api.family: Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform publisher-model video API, formerly Vertex AI
api.callback: no provider callback commitment was established
api.polling: poll the provider operation with a durable lease and bounded stop conditions
api.cancellation: must be confirmed against the current operation endpoint before enablement
api.artifactHandling: copy final MP4 into governed storage before exposing it

pricing.unit: second of generated video with audio
pricing.notes: Veo 3.1 Fast public list price for 720p output.

rights.outputOwnership: Generated Output is Customer Data and Google does not assert ownership in new IP created in it.
rights.trainingUse: Google does not train or fine-tune models on Customer Data without prior permission or instruction.
restrictions: confirm Veo is covered by generated-output indemnity

data.defaultRetention: Request/response logging is off by default; abuse-monitoring prompt logging may apply. In-memory caching can last up to 24 hours and can be disabled.
data.zeroRetention: Possible only after all documented abuse-monitoring, advanced-safety, logging, and cache conditions are satisfied.
data.residency: The GA Veo 3.1 model card documents data residency and us-central1 availability.

regions.productionCondition: Use the documented US region and complete cross-border/data-location review.

limits.published: The GA Veo 3.1 model card lists 50 regional online prediction requests per minute per base model.

sla.publicCommitment: The reviewed Vertex AI SLA did not clearly list the Google publisher generative-video model as a covered service; Preview features are excluded.
sla.productionCondition: Confirm model-specific SLA/support and whether Provisioned Throughput is required.

goLiveConditions: Google Cloud agreement, DPA, generated-output indemnity coverage, region, and SLA are approved
abuse logging, zero-retention exception, request logging, and cache settings are recorded
operation polling, cancellation behavior, timeouts, C2PA preservation, and artifact copy pass staging
quota, Provisioned Throughput decision, budget cap, and kill switch are recorded
the exact real-call approval package is completed

api.callback: no webhook commitment was established from the reviewed public guide
api.cancellation: verify the current task cancellation endpoint before enablement
api.artifactHandling: download ephemeral output URLs into governed storage within 24-48 hours

pricing.notes: Gen-4.5 costs 12 credits per second and API credits cost USD 0.01 each.

rights.outputOwnership: Runway does not claim ownership and does not restrict commercial Output use under the public terms.
rights.trainingUse: Public terms grant Runway a perpetual license and allow Inputs and Outputs to train and improve models.
restrictions: Powered by Runway attribution applies to API applications
public training terms are unacceptable for sensitive production content

data.defaultRetention: Ephemeral uploads expire after 24 hours and output URLs expire within 24-48 hours; broader task/content retention is not clearly stated in the reviewed public docs.
data.zeroRetention: No public zero-retention control was established.
data.residency: No customer-selectable residency commitment was established; public terms describe US-controlled service.

documented: international access subject to local law and provider eligibility
regions.productionCondition: Legal must approve access geography and data transfers.

limits.published: Tier 1: 1 concurrent video task, 50 generations per rolling day, and USD 100 monthly spend; higher tiers increase automatically with purchases.

sla.publicCommitment: Public terms do not warrant uninterrupted service; guaranteed minimum concurrency requires an enterprise exception.
sla.productionCondition: Obtain written SLA/support, guaranteed concurrency, retention, and no-training terms.

goLiveConditions: enterprise terms remove or adequately limit training use and define retention/deletion
Powered by Runway attribution and end-user flow-down are accepted by product/legal
region, DPA, SLA, guaranteed concurrency, and support are recorded
task polling, cancellation, throttled state, and ephemeral artifact capture pass staging
an independent budget and exact real-call approval are recorded

api.cancellation: abort the request/stream; application job cancellation remains authoritative
api.artifactHandling: stream or copy the MP3 response into governed storage

pricing.notes: Public API list price; enterprise contract pricing can differ. V1 caps tracks at 3 minutes even though the API parameter permits longer values.

rights.outputOwnership: Customer retains Output rights as between the parties, subject to Music and model-specific terms.
rights.trainingUse: Public terms permit model-improvement use unless the account opts out; prior uses are unaffected.
restrictions: self-serve plans prohibit reseller and music-library rights
full film, TV, radio, and Studio Games rights require Enterprise Music
plan eligibility depends on organization size
streaming and media rights vary by plan

data.defaultRetention: Standard customer data is hosted in the US; the reviewed public page did not state a default Music-content deletion period.
data.zeroRetention: Optional for eligible enterprise configurations; Music availability under ZRM must be confirmed.
data.residency: Enterprise isolated environments are offered in the EU, India, and Singapore; processing can still leave the region unless stricter conditions apply.

regions.productionCondition: Select and contract an eligible region before integration.

limits.published: Music concurrency is 2 on Starter/Creator/Pro, 5 on Scale/Business/Enterprise Music Lite, and 10+ on Enterprise Music.

sla.publicCommitment: Music terms provide the service as-is; enterprise pricing advertises custom DPA/SLA terms.
sla.productionCondition: Enterprise Music must include the agreed SLA, support, deletion, and region terms.

goLiveConditions: Enterprise Music contract grants reseller, streaming, media, library/repository, and required API rights
DPA, retention/deletion, no-training opt-out, region, and ZRM model eligibility are recorded
SLA, support, price, concurrency, and organization eligibility are confirmed
copyright filters, license metadata, streaming, abort, artifact storage, and download controls pass staging
the exact real-call approval package is completed

api.family: Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform Interactions API
api.polling: adapter must confirm whether nonterminal interaction states require polling
api.cancellation: not established for the Preview interaction flow
api.artifactHandling: decode returned audio and persist immediately in governed storage

pricing.notes: Public Preview list price; price and model behavior can change without GA notice.

rights.outputOwnership: Preview documentation expressly allows production/commercial use and third-party disclosure; Generated Output is Customer Data and Google asserts no ownership in new IP.
rights.trainingUse: Google does not train or fine-tune models on Customer Data without prior permission or instruction.
restrictions: commercial/product behavior can change or be discontinued

data.defaultRetention: General Agent Platform abuse-monitoring and cache rules apply; Preview-specific Lyria retention was not separately established.
data.zeroRetention: Requires model-specific confirmation; some advanced AI features cannot achieve ZDR.
data.residency: The Interactions example uses a global location; Preview data-location guarantees require written confirmation.

documented: global API location in the reviewed interaction example
regions.productionCondition: Accept cross-border processing only after legal and contract review.

limits.published: No stable public Lyria 3 Pro quota was established from the reviewed model page.

sla.publicCommitment: Google Pre-GA terms expressly exclude SLA and indemnity.
sla.productionCondition: Backup use requires a signed acceptance of Preview risk and a migration/disable plan.

goLiveConditions: product/legal explicitly accept Pre-GA no-SLA and no-indemnity risk
region, quota, retention, ZDR eligibility, model lifecycle, and interaction polling are confirmed
commercial output rights and user-facing disclosures are approved
quality, safety, latency, budget, and migration tests pass
an independent exact real-call approval is completed

rejectedCandidates.reason: OpenAI announced removal of the Videos API, sora-2, and sora-2-pro on 2026-09-24 with no recommended replacement.
Lyria 3 remains Preview and has no SLA or Google indemnity; it is retained only as a conditional backup.
Replicate aligns with the existing fixture shell but has an extra model-license layer and no public SLA; it remains the image backup while the clearer OpenAI rights/data path is primary.

blockingConditions: No provider is production-approved by this decision record.
Every provider requires an exact staging real-call approval before the first external request.
ElevenLabs Music requires an Enterprise Music contract that grants platform/reseller and complete required media rights.
Runway requires enterprise no-training, retention/deletion, residency, SLA, and support terms before backup enablement.
Google must confirm Veo model-specific SLA/indemnity coverage and Lyria Preview region/quota/retention behavior.
OpenAI must confirm production-project supported geography, gpt-image-2 residency, and selected retention controls.
Anthropic must be accepted with US storage or replaced by a contracted regional deployment.
Provider price, model availability, account quota, legal terms, and supported regions must be rechecked within 30 days of production launch.
